# services.homer

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.homer`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.homer.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable A dead simple static HOMepage for your servER to keep your services on hand, from a simple yaml configuration file. . |
| `services.homer.package` | `package` | The homer package to use. |
| `services.homer.settings` | `YAML 1.1 value` | Settings serialized into {file}`config.yml` before build. If left empty, the default configuration shipped with the package will be used instead. For more information, see the [official documentation](https://github.com/bastienwirtz/homer/blob/main/docs/configuration.md). Note that the full configuration will be written to the nix store as world readable, which may include secrets such as [api-keys](https://github.com/bastienwirtz/homer/blob/main/docs/customservices.md). To add files such as icons or backgrounds, you can reference them in line such as `nix icon = "${./icon.png}"; ` This will add the file to the nix store upon build, referencing it by file path as expected by Homer. |
| `services.homer.virtualHost.caddy.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable a virtualhost to serve homer through caddy. |
| `services.homer.virtualHost.domain` | `string` | Domain to use for the virtual host. This can be used to change nginx options like `nix services.nginx.virtualHosts."$\{config.services.homer.virtualHost.domain}".listen = [ ... ] ` or `nix services.nginx.virtualHosts."example.com".listen = [ ... ] ` |
| `services.homer.virtualHost.nginx.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable a virtualhost to serve homer through nginx. |
